title: Angostura martiana
intro: Angostura martiana, also known as Guayacan, is a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Angostura martiana include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional folk remedies and medicines.
taxonomy: Angostura martiana is classified within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification is due to its morphological and chemical characteristics, which are similar to other species within the Rutaceae family. The genus Angostura is a small group of plants, and within it, Angostura martiana is one of the several species. Further classification within the Rutaceae family involves the angiosperm order Sapindales. The specific classification of Angostura martiana is Angostura martiana (Kunth) B. L. Rob.
